patient_message: I've had a mild headache since this morning. It's maybe a 2 out of 10. Probably just dehydration.
patient_history: Patient underwent surgical clipping of a cerebral aneurysm 3 weeks ago. Discharged with instructions to report any new headache immediately as a warning sign of re-bleed.
clinical_reasoning: New headache post-aneurysm surgery = sentinel bleed until proven otherwise

patient_message: I noticed a small bruise on my forearm. I don't remember bumping into anything.
patient_history: Patient has severe hemophilia A (Factor VIII deficiency). Last hospitalization was for spontaneous intramuscular bleed. On prophylactic factor replacement therapy.
clinical_reasoning: Spontaneous bruising in hemophiliac = possible internal bleed requiring factor infusion

patient_message: Feeling a little tired today, maybe slightly more than usual. Probably just didn't sleep well.
patient_history: Patient received allogeneic bone marrow transplant 6 weeks ago. Currently in immunosuppressed recovery phase. Last CBC showed neutrophil count of 0.3 (severely neutropenic).
clinical_reasoning: Fatigue in neutropenic post-transplant patient = possible neutropenic sepsis, an oncologic emergency

patient_message: I have a mild low-grade fever, around 99.8. I feel okay otherwise.
patient_history: Patient is on FOLFOX chemotherapy for stage III colon cancer. Last white blood cell count from 5 days ago showed ANC of 0.4. Neutropenic precautions in place.
clinical_reasoning: Fever >= 38C in neutropenic chemo patient = neutropenic fever, treated as emergency per oncology protocol

patient_message: Slight chest tightness, maybe a 1 or 2 out of 10. Comes and goes. I've been stressed at work.
patient_history: Patient had a drug-eluting coronary stent placed in the LAD artery 8 days ago. On dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin + Plavix). Premature stent discontinuation risk is high in this window.
clinical_reasoning: Any chest symptom within 30 days of stent placement = possible acute stent thrombosis, mortality risk >50%

patient_message: My leg is a little swollen around the calf. Not painful really, just puffy.
patient_history: Patient had total hip replacement 10 days ago. Not yet mobile, on prophylactic enoxaparin. Has a history of prior DVT in the same leg.
clinical_reasoning: Unilateral calf swelling post-surgery in DVT-history patient = high probability DVT, PE risk

patient_message: Feeling a bit confused and foggy this afternoon. Probably just tired.
patient_history: Patient has a known glioblastoma multiforme (GBM, grade IV brain tumor) in the right frontal lobe. Completed radiation 2 months ago. On dexamethasone taper.
clinical_reasoning: New confusion in GBM patient = possible tumor progression, cerebral edema, or herniation

patient_message: I have a mild nosebleed that started about 20 minutes ago. It's mostly stopped now.
patient_history: Patient is on warfarin for mechanical heart valve. Last INR checked 3 days ago was 4.8 (target 2.5-3.5). Dose adjustment was pending.
clinical_reasoning: Bleeding in supratherapeutic anticoagulated patient = risk of serious internal hemorrhage

patient_message: Mild shortness of breath when walking up the stairs today. A little more than usual.
patient_history: Patient has severe pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH), WHO Class III. On IV epoprostenol via central line. Any acute decompensation can rapidly become fatal.
clinical_reasoning: New exertional dyspnea in PAH patient = early right heart decompensation, can deteriorate within hours

patient_message: My back is a little achy today, like a mild muscle strain. Probably from sitting too long.
patient_history: Patient received a cadaveric kidney transplant 5 weeks ago. On tacrolimus and mycophenolate. Transplanted kidney is in the retroperitoneal left flank area.
clinical_reasoning: Back/flank pain in renal transplant patient = possible graft rejection or vascular complication

patient_message: Slight sore throat, bit of a scratch feeling. Nothing major.
patient_history: Patient is on clozapine for treatment-resistant schizophrenia. Mandatory ANC monitoring. Last ANC was borderline at 1.4. Clozapine carries a 1-2% risk of agranulocytosis.
clinical_reasoning: Sore throat in clozapine patient = first sign of agranulocytosis, requires immediate ANC check and possible drug cessation

patient_message: Feeling a bit off today, kind of weak and nauseous. Probably ate something bad.
patient_history: Patient has primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison's disease). Was recently under significant physical stress (flu last week). On hydrocortisone replacement therapy but has been vomiting.
clinical_reasoning: Weakness/nausea + vomiting in Addison's patient under stress = possible adrenal crisis, can be fatal within hours

patient_message: Mild headache and I feel a little lightheaded. Maybe just dehydrated.
patient_history: Patient is on lithium 900mg/day for bipolar disorder. Has chronic kidney disease stage 2, which reduces lithium clearance. Last lithium level was 1.3 mEq/L (upper limit of normal).
clinical_reasoning: Headache + dizziness in lithium patient with CKD = possible early lithium toxicity

patient_message: A little difficulty swallowing today, feels slightly tight when I swallow.
patient_history: Patient had a total thyroidectomy with parathyroid exploration 48 hours ago. Post-operative hematoma is a known complication in this window.
clinical_reasoning: Dysphagia 48h post-thyroidectomy = expanding neck hematoma compressing airway — surgical emergency

patient_message: Mild blurry vision in my right eye today. It cleared up after a few minutes.
patient_history: Patient has biopsy-confirmed giant cell arteritis (temporal arteritis) diagnosed 2 months ago. Currently tapering prednisone from 60mg to 20mg. Visual symptoms are a major red flag for this condition.
clinical_reasoning: Transient vision loss in GCA patient on steroid taper = threatened permanent blindness from optic artery involvement

patient_message: Minor gum bleeding when I brushed my teeth this morning. Happens occasionally.
patient_history: Patient had a coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) 2 weeks ago. On dual antiplatelet therapy. Also has known von Willebrand disease type 2, a platelet function disorder.
clinical_reasoning: Bleeding in post-CABG patient with von Willebrand disease on antiplatelets = compounding coagulopathy risk

patient_message: Slight numbness and tingling in my fingers, started today. Probably slept on my arm funny.
patient_history: Patient had anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) at C5-C6 three days ago. New neurological symptoms in this post-op window are a red flag for spinal cord compression.
clinical_reasoning: New neurology post-spinal surgery = possible hematoma or hardware complication causing cord compression

patient_message: Mild stomach cramping today, maybe a 2 or 3 out of 10. Could be gas.
patient_history: Patient had a bowel resection and anastomosis for Crohn's disease 6 days ago. Anastomotic leak is most common between post-operative days 3-7.
clinical_reasoning: Abdominal pain in peak anastomotic leak window post-bowel surgery = possible peritonitis

patient_message: Feeling a bit anxious and heart is fluttering a bit. I've been stressed about work.
patient_history: Patient has a known pheochromocytoma (adrenal gland tumor) confirmed on imaging. Awaiting surgery in 3 weeks. Not yet on alpha-blockade preoperative preparation.
clinical_reasoning: Palpitations in unblocked pheochromocytoma patient = possible hypertensive crisis from catecholamine surge

patient_message: I have a small rash on my forearm, appeared overnight. It's not itchy.
patient_history: Patient was started on lamotrigine 3 weeks ago for epilepsy. Any new rash within 8 weeks of starting lamotrigine must be evaluated as possible Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS), a life-threatening skin reaction.
clinical_reasoning: New rash within SJS window for lamotrigine = must rule out SJS/TEN until proven otherwise

patient_message: Slight cough started today, dry and mild. Probably just the dry weather.
patient_history: Patient received a bilateral lung transplant 7 weeks ago. On tacrolimus and mycophenolate. Any respiratory symptoms in this window are concerning for primary graft dysfunction or acute rejection.
clinical_reasoning: New cough in early post-lung transplant window = acute rejection or infection until proven otherwise

patient_message: Mild ankle swelling today, one side. Not painful.
patient_history: Patient has decompensated liver cirrhosis (Child-Pugh Class C) with known esophageal varices. Albumin is critically low at 2.0. Any volume shift can precipitate hepatic decompensation.
clinical_reasoning: New edema in decompensated cirrhosis = possible hypoalbuminemia worsening or portal hypertension escalation

patient_message: Mild joint pain in my knees. Maybe from walking more than usual.
patient_history: Patient has systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with history of lupus nephritis class IV. Recently tapered off steroids at own request. Lupus flares can rapidly progress to renal failure.
clinical_reasoning: Joint pain in SLE patient post-steroid taper = possible lupus flare with nephritis risk

patient_message: Slight ear pain in my left ear, mild discomfort. Probably just earwax.
patient_history: Patient has a cochlear implant in the left ear placed 3 months ago. Device infection is a serious complication that can lead to meningitis and device failure.
clinical_reasoning: Ear pain over cochlear implant site = possible device infection, risk of meningitis

patient_message: I feel a little tired and have a very mild headache. It's been a long week.
patient_history: Patient received a heart transplant 5 weeks ago. On triple immunosuppression. Fatigue and headache can be the only presenting symptoms of acute cardiac rejection in this early window.
clinical_reasoning: Fatigue + headache 5 weeks post-heart transplant = possible acute rejection, graft function must be assessed urgently

patient_message: Minor headache after I bumped my head on a cabinet. It barely hurt when it happened.
patient_history: Patient is on rivaroxaban (Xarelto) 20mg daily for atrial fibrillation. Has a platelet count of 60,000 due to ITP (immune thrombocytopenic purpura). Compounding coagulopathy.
clinical_reasoning: Head trauma in anticoagulated thrombocytopenic patient = high risk of intracranial hemorrhage even from minor impact

patient_message: Mild stomach discomfort and lost my appetite today. Probably just not hungry.
patient_history: Patient has a ventricular assist device (LVAD) implanted for end-stage heart failure. On warfarin. GI symptoms in LVAD patients are associated with GI angiodysplasia bleeding, a known LVAD complication.
clinical_reasoning: GI symptoms in LVAD patient on anticoagulation = possible GI bleed from angiodysplasia, common and serious in LVAD

patient_message: A small cut on my finger from cooking. It's bleeding a little but it's tiny.
patient_history: Patient has Glanzmann thrombasthenia, a rare platelet function disorder where platelets cannot aggregate. Even minor wounds can result in uncontrolled hemorrhage without platelet transfusion.
clinical_reasoning: Any bleeding in Glanzmann thrombasthenia = cannot clot normally, requires platelet transfusion

patient_message: Mild lower abdominal pressure, not really pain. Comes and goes.
patient_history: Patient had an abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) repaired with an endovascular stent graft (EVAR) 3 weeks ago. An endoleak (blood leaking around the graft) is most common in this early post-op period.
clinical_reasoning: Abdominal symptoms post-EVAR = possible endoleak or graft migration, can lead to aneurysm rupture

patient_message: Feeling slightly dizzy when I stand up quickly. Just happened once or twice today.
patient_history: Patient has hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy (HOCM) with severe outflow tract obstruction. Was recently started on a new antihypertensive by another provider. HOCM patients are preload-dependent and vasodilators can cause sudden collapse.
clinical_reasoning: Orthostatic symptoms in HOCM patient on new vasodilator = possible outflow tract dynamic obstruction worsening — syncope risk
